introduction: The OAuth Provider Registry provides a pluggable identity provider abstraction for apikit-based services, enabling users to authenticate via OAuth providers (initially GitHub) through two public endpoints: `GET /auth/providers` and `POST /auth/callback`. Note: requirements referencing `db.WithTx`, `db.WrapError`, `db.FormatTime`, `APIError`, `CacheMiddleware`, `CachePublic`, `FormatUTC`, `NowUTC`, and `APIGroup` APIs are based on upstream spec interfaces described in the PRD and should be cross-checked against spec `01_server_core` and spec `02_database_layer` implementations before coding.

glossary.APIError: The function from spec `01_server_core` that constructs a standard error response envelope.
glossary.APIGroup: The function from spec `01_server_core` that returns the Echo router group on which API handlers are registered.
glossary.CacheMiddleware: The Echo middleware from spec `01_server_core` that sets `Cache-Control` headers on responses.
glossary.CachePublic: A constant passed to `CacheMiddleware` that sets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` on the response.
glossary.FormatUTC: The function from spec `01_server_core` that formats a UTC timestamp as an RFC 3339 string.
glossary.NowUTC: The function from spec `01_server_core` that returns the current time in UTC.
glossary.OAuthConfig: The Go struct holding the parsed `[[oauth.providers]]` TOML configuration section.
glossary.Provider: An OAuth identity provider (e.g. GitHub) that implements the `Provider` interface for authentication.
glossary.Provider interface: The Go interface (`Provider`) defining `Name()`, `AuthorizeURL()`, `Exchange()`, and `UserInfo()` methods.
glossary.ProviderConfig: The Go struct representing a single provider entry in the `[[oauth.providers]]` TOML configuration array.
glossary.Registry: The `oauth.Registry` type that holds all configured providers, keyed by name.
glossary.TokenPrefix: The build-time configurable prefix string prepended to API keys, e.g. `ak` producing keys of the form `ak_<key_id>_<secret>`.
glossary.UserInfo: The struct returned by `Provider.UserInfo()` containing `Username`, `Email`, and `ProviderID` fields.
glossary.admin_config: A database table populated at runtime by the `admin_bootstrap` spec that stores the designated admin email address.
glossary.admin_email: The email address stored in `admin_config` that designates which user becomes the first admin on initial OAuth login.
glossary.api_keys: The database table storing API key records including `key_id`, `secret_hash`, `expires_at`, and `revoked_at` columns.
glossary.authorize_url: The URL the user's browser is redirected to for OAuth authorization. GitHub default: `https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize`.
glossary.db.FormatTime: Database layer function from spec `02_database_layer` that formats a time value for storage.
glossary.db.WithTx: Database layer function from spec `02_database_layer` that runs a function within a database transaction.
glossary.db.WrapError: Database layer function from spec `02_database_layer` that maps SQLite error codes to sentinel errors.
glossary.external_url: The public-facing URL of the apikit server, configured in `config.toml`, used to validate production redirect URIs.
glossary.httptest.Server: A Go standard library test helper that runs a local HTTP server for testing HTTP client code.
glossary.key_id: The random 8-character alphanumeric identifier portion of an API key, stored in plaintext in the `api_keys` table.
glossary.last-write-wins: The concurrency resolution strategy for simultaneous OAuth logins: SQLite's single-connection serialization ensures transactions execute sequentially; the final transaction's key is the one the user retains.
glossary.provider_id: The identity provider's own numeric or string identifier for a user (e.g. GitHub's numeric user ID converted to string), stored in the `users` table.
glossary.redirect_uri: The URL the identity provider redirects back to after authorization. Must match the configured allowlist.
glossary.revoked_at: A timestamp column in the `api_keys` table indicating when a key was revoked. Null means the key is still active.
glossary.scope: The OAuth permission scope(s) requested from the identity provider during authorization. The GitHub provider hardcodes `user:email`.
glossary.secret: The random 32-character alphanumeric portion of an API key, stored only as a SHA-256 hash. The plaintext is returned once at creation.
glossary.secret_hash: The SHA-256 hash of the API key secret stored in the `api_keys` table. The plaintext secret is never stored.
glossary.token_url: The URL used server-to-server to exchange an authorization code for an access token. GitHub default: `https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token`.
glossary.upsert: A database operation that inserts a new row if no match exists, or updates the existing row if a match is found. Used for user records matched by `(provider, provider_id)`.
glossary.userinfo_url: The URL used server-to-server to retrieve user identity information using the access token. GitHub default: `https://api.github.com/user`.
glossary.users: The database table storing user records with columns including `id`, `username`, `email`, `status`, `role`, `provider`, `provider_id`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`.
glossary.well-known URLs: The standard OAuth endpoint URLs for a provider (authorize, token, userinfo) that are built into the provider implementation as defaults.
glossary.Name() string: Provider interface method that returns the provider's identifier string (e.g. `"github"`).
glossary.AuthorizeURL(state, redirectURI string): Provider interface method that constructs and returns the full OAuth authorization URL with state and redirect URI parameters.
Provider interface method signature for constructing the OAuth authorization URL.
glossary.UserInfo(ctx context.Context, accessToke: Provider interface method that retrieves user identity information from the identity provider using the given access token.
glossary.Exchange(ctx context.Context, code, redi: Provider interface method that exchanges an authorization code for an access token from the identity provider.
glossary.ProviderID string: Field in the `UserInfo` struct holding the provider-specific user identifier (e.g. GitHub numeric ID as string).
glossary.Email string: Field in the `UserInfo` struct holding the user's email address (required; empty triggers login failure).
glossary.Username string: Field in the `UserInfo` struct holding the provider's username / login.
glossary.type UserInfo = oauth.UserInfo: Go type alias re-exporting `oauth.UserInfo` through the root `apikit` package.
glossary.type Provider = oauth.Provider: Go type alias re-exporting `oauth.Provider` through the root `apikit` package.
glossary.internal/oauth: Internal Go package (`github.com/txsvc/apikit/internal/oauth`) implementing the OAuth provider registry and handlers.
glossary.*Registry: Pointer to the `Registry` type that holds all configured OAuth providers.
glossary.NewRegistry(): Constructor function that creates an empty `Registry`.
glossary.p.Name(): Call to the `Name()` method on a `Provider` instance, returning its identifier string.
glossary.Register(p Provider): Registry method that adds a provider to the registry, returning an error if the name is already registered.
glossary.nil: Go's zero value for pointers, interfaces, maps, slices, channels, and function types.
glossary.Get(name string): Registry method that retrieves a provider by name, returning `nil` if not found.
glossary.Get(name): Short-form call to the Registry's `Get` method.
glossary.List(): Registry method that returns all registered provider names in alphabetical order.
glossary.https://github.com/login/oauth/access_to: GitHub's default OAuth token exchange URL.
glossary.https://api.github.com/user: GitHub's default user info API URL.
glossary.Accept: application/json: HTTP header requesting JSON response format, set on GitHub token exchange requests.
glossary.Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>: HTTP header carrying the OAuth access token for user info retrieval.
glossary.login: GitHub API field name for the user's username.
glossary.id: GitHub API field name for the numeric user identifier.
glossary.email: GitHub API field name or general term for the user's email address.
glossary.access_token: The OAuth access token returned by the identity provider's token endpoint.
glossary.error: JSON field name in the identity provider's error response, or the API error envelope key.
glossary.name == \"github\": Condition matching the GitHub provider name during config-driven registration.
glossary.Register(): Short-form reference to the `Register(p Provider)` method on the Registry.
glossary.(nil, error): Go return pattern indicating failure: nil value and non-nil error.
glossary.[[oauth.providers]]: TOML array-of-tables syntax for configuring multiple OAuth providers in `config.toml`.
glossary.OAuth OAuthConfig: Config struct field holding the OAuth configuration, tagged with `toml:"oauth"`.
glossary.Providers []ProviderConfig: OAuthConfig struct field holding the slice of provider configurations.
glossary.ClientID: Field in `ProviderConfig` holding the OAuth client ID.
glossary.ClientSecret: Field in `ProviderConfig` holding the OAuth client secret.
glossary.AuthorizeURL: Field in `ProviderConfig` for the optional authorize URL override, or the Provider interface method name.
glossary.TokenURL: Field in `ProviderConfig` for the optional token URL override.
glossary.UserinfoURL: Field in `ProviderConfig` for the optional user info URL override.
glossary.Name: Field in `ProviderConfig` holding the provider name, or the Provider interface method.
glossary.name: The provider name field in the config or request body.
glossary.client_id: The OAuth client ID field in the TOML config.
glossary.client_secret: The OAuth client secret field in the TOML config.
glossary.toml:\"oauth\": Go struct tag binding the `OAuth` field to the `[oauth]` TOML section.
glossary.toml:\"providers\": Go struct tag binding the `Providers` field to the `[[oauth.providers]]` TOML array.
glossary.GET /auth/providers: Public endpoint that lists configured OAuth providers (name and authorize URL).
glossary.POST /auth/callback: Public endpoint that exchanges an authorization code for a user record and API key.
glossary.{mount_point}/auth/providers: Full path for the provider list endpoint, prefixed with the configurable mount point.
glossary.CacheMiddleware(CachePublic): Echo middleware call applying `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` to the route.
glossary.Cache-Control: public, max-age=300: HTTP cache header value for static discovery endpoints (5-minute public cache).
glossary.Cache-Control: no-store: HTTP cache header value for mutable resources (no caching).
glossary.Registry.Get(name).AuthorizeURL(\"\", \": Call chain retrieving a provider and constructing its authorize URL (used in the providers endpoint).
glossary.scope=user:email: GitHub OAuth scope parameter requesting access to the user's email address.
glossary.state: A cryptographically random parameter included in OAuth authorization URLs for CSRF protection.
glossary.provider: The provider name field in the callback request body.
glossary.code: The authorization code field in the callback request body.
glossary.expires: The API key expiry field in the callback request body (0, 30, 60, or 90 days).
glossary.user: The user object in the callback response, or the JSON key for user data.
glossary.api_key: The API key object in the callback response containing `key`, `key_id`, and `expires_at`.
glossary.updated_at: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp indicating when a resource was last modified.
glossary.api_key.expires_at: The API key's expiration timestamp (RFC 3339 UTC or null for non-expiring keys).
glossary.\"provider is required\": Error message returned when the `provider` field is missing from the callback request.
glossary.\"code is required\": Error message returned when the `code` field is missing from the callback request.
glossary.\"redirect_uri is required\": Error message returned when the `redirect_uri` field is missing from the callback request.
glossary.\"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90\": Error message returned when the `expires` field has an invalid value.
glossary.\"unknown provider: <name>\": Error message returned when the provider name doesn't match any registered provider.
glossary.\"redirect_uri is not allowed\": Error message returned when the redirect URI doesn't match the configured allowlist.
glossary.\"authorization code exchange failed\": Error message returned when the code exchange with the identity provider fails.
glossary.\"failed to retrieve user info from prov: Error message returned when the user info API call to the identity provider fails.
glossary.\"provider returned empty email; email i: Error message returned when the identity provider returns a null or empty email.
glossary.\"user is blocked\": Error message returned when a blocked user attempts to authenticate via OAuth.
glossary.\"internal server error\": Error message returned for database or other internal failures during the callback flow.
glossary.APIError(): Helper function from spec `01_server_core` that writes a standard JSON error envelope.
glossary.APIGroup(): Server method from spec `01_server_core` that returns the Echo route group at the configured mount point.
glossary.http://localhost:*: Wildcard pattern matching any localhost URL with any port (development redirect URI).
glossary.http://localhost:<port>/*: URL pattern matching localhost with any valid port and path (development redirect URI allowlist).
glossary.<port>: A valid TCP port number in the redirect URI.
glossary.Exchange(ctx, code, redirectURI): Short-form call to the `Exchange` method on a `Provider` instance.
glossary.provider.Exchange(ctx, code, redirectURI: Call to the provider's `Exchange` method with context, authorization code, and redirect URI.
glossary.provider.Exchange(): Short-form reference to calling the `Exchange` method on a provider.
glossary.accessToken: The OAuth access token obtained from the identity provider's token endpoint.
glossary.provider.UserInfo(ctx, accessToken): Call to the provider's `UserInfo` method with context and access token.
glossary.provider.UserInfo(): Short-form reference to calling the `UserInfo` method on a provider.
glossary.*UserInfo: Pointer to the `UserInfo` struct returned by the provider's `UserInfo` method.
glossary.Email: The email field on the `UserInfo` struct (required; empty triggers login failure).
glossary.(provider, provider_id): The composite unique key on the `users` table used to match existing users during OAuth upsert.
glossary.status = 'active': SQL value indicating the user account is active.
glossary.role = 'admin': SQL value indicating the user has the admin role.
glossary.role = 'user': SQL value indicating the user has the default user role.
glossary.NowUTC(): Utility function from spec `01_server_core` that returns the current time as RFC 3339 UTC with Z suffix.
glossary.FormatUTC(): Utility function from spec `01_server_core` that formats a `time.Time` as RFC 3339 UTC with Z suffix.
glossary.NowUTC() + (expires * 24h): Formula for computing `expires_at`: current UTC time plus the expiry duration in days.
glossary.expires == 0: Condition where the API key has no expiration (indefinite).
glossary.expires_at: Nullable timestamp field for API key expiration (null when `expires` is 0).
glossary.revoked_at = NowUTC(): Setting the `revoked_at` field to the current UTC timestamp when revoking a key.
glossary.blocked: User status value indicating the account is blocked.
glossary.null: JSON null value (used for nullable fields like `expires_at` when expires is 0).
glossary.crypto/rand: Go standard library package used for cryptographically secure random number generation.
glossary.math/rand: Go standard library package for pseudo-random numbers; explicitly not used for security-sensitive generation.
glossary.*http.Client: Go standard library HTTP client type used for identity provider API calls.
glossary.http.DefaultTransport: Go standard library default HTTP transport with no custom TLS configuration.
glossary.RegisterOAuthHandlers(group *echo.Group,: Internal function that registers the OAuth endpoints on the Echo group during server bootstrap.
glossary.<TokenPrefix>_<key_id>_<secret>: API key format: configurable prefix, 8-char random key ID, and 32-char random secret.
glossary.apikit.TokenPrefix: The build-time configurable token prefix variable (default `ak`) from the root `apikit` package.
glossary.\"ak\": Default value of the `apikit.TokenPrefix` build-time variable.
glossary.SetMaxOpenConns(1): Go `*sql.DB` method setting the maximum number of open database connections to 1.
glossary.database_layer: Spec `02_database_layer` providing the SQLite database layer.
glossary.\"gitlab\": Example of an unknown provider name that would cause a startup error.
glossary.UserInfo(ctx, accessToken): Short-form call to the `UserInfo` method on a `Provider` instance.
glossary.'user': String literal for the default user role value.
glossary.'blocked': String literal for the blocked user status value.
glossary.status = 'blocked': SQL condition matching users with blocked status.

user_story.goal: define a common interface for OAuth identity providers
user_story.benefit: so that new providers can be added without modifying handlers or middleware

acceptance_criteria.action: defines the `Provider` interface with four methods: `Name() string`, `AuthorizeURL(state, redirectURI string) string`, `Exchange(ctx context.Context, code, redirectURI string) (string, error)`, and `UserInfo(ctx context.Context, accessToken string) (*UserInfo, error)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Compilation succeeds; any type that implements all four methods satisfies the interface

acceptance_criteria.action: defines the `UserInfo` struct with fields `Username string`, `Email string`, and `ProviderID string`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Struct is exported and usable as the return type of `Provider.UserInfo()`

acceptance_criteria.action: re-exports `Provider` as a type alias `type Provider = oauth.Provider` and `UserInfo` as a type alias `type UserInfo = oauth.UserInfo` so consuming projects can implement custom providers without importing `internal/oauth` directly
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Consuming projects can assign `oauth.Provider` and `apikit.Provider` interchangeably

edge_cases.action: rejects the type at compile time with a missing-method error; no runtime check is needed
edge_cases.return_contract: Compilation fails with a Go interface-satisfaction error
edge_cases.error_condition: a type implements only a subset of the four `Provider` interface methods

user_story.goal: maintain a keyed collection of registered OAuth providers
user_story.benefit: so that handlers can look up the correct provider by name at request time

acceptance_criteria.action: is created via `NewRegistry()` returning an empty `*Registry` with no registered providers
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `NewRegistry()` returns a non-nil `*Registry`; `List()` returns an empty slice

acceptance_criteria.action: adds the provider to the internal map keyed by `p.Name()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `nil` error; subsequent `Get(p.Name())` returns the registered provider
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `Register(p Provider)` is called with a provider whose name is not already in the registry

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the `Provider` registered under that name, or `nil` if no provider with that name exists
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `Provider` or `nil`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `Get(name string)` is called

acceptance_criteria.action: returns all registered provider names sorted in alphabetical order
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `[]string` sorted lexicographically ascending
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `List()` is called

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error describing the duplicate name; the existing provider is not replaced
edge_cases.return_contract: Returns `error` with message indicating duplicate provider name; registry state unchanged
edge_cases.error_condition: `Register(p Provider)` is called with a provider whose name is already registered

edge_cases.action: returns `nil` because no provider is registered under an empty name
edge_cases.error_condition: `Get(name)` is called with an empty string

user_story.goal: authenticate users via GitHub OAuth with built-in well-known URLs and optional URL overrides
user_story.benefit: so that GitHub is supported out of the box including GitHub Enterprise deployments

acceptance_criteria.action: implements the `Provider` interface with default `authorize_url` `https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize`, default `token_url` `https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token`, and default `userinfo_url` `https://api.github.com/user`; when config override fields are non-empty they replace the respective defaults entirely
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `Name()` returns `"github"`

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs and returns the authorization URL by appending `client_id`, `scope=user:email`, `state`, and `redirect_uri` as query parameters to the configured `authorize_url`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns a string URL with all four query parameters present
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `AuthorizeURL(state, redirectURI string)` is called

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to `token_url` with form-encoded body containing `client_id`, `client_secret`, `code`, and `redirect_uri`; sets `Accept: application/json` header; parses the JSON response to extract `access_token`; uses the shared `*http.Client` with 30-second timeout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `(accessToken string, nil)` on success
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `Exchange(ctx, code, redirectURI)` is called

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to `userinfo_url` with `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` header; parses JSON response extracting `login` → `UserInfo.Username`, `email` → `UserInfo.Email`, and `id` (numeric) → `UserInfo.ProviderID` (converted to string); uses the shared `*http.Client`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `(*UserInfo, nil)` on success with all three fields populated
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `UserInfo(ctx, accessToken)` is called

acceptance_criteria.action: receives the shared `*http.Client` via constructor injection at creation time and does not create its own HTTP client
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Provider struct holds a reference to the injected `*http.Client`

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error from `Exchange`; the error message includes the provider error value
edge_cases.error_condition: the token endpoint returns a JSON response containing an `error` field

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error from `Exchange` indicating the token was not returned
edge_cases.error_condition: the token endpoint returns a JSON response with `access_token` empty or absent

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error from the respective method; no retry is attempted; the shared `*http.Client` 30-second timeout cancels the pending request and returns a timeout error
edge_cases.return_contract: Returns an error; caller receives a wrapped I/O or HTTP error
edge_cases.error_condition: the HTTP call to `token_url` or `userinfo_url` times out or returns a non-2xx status

edge_cases.action: propagates the context cancellation error without hanging; the underlying `*http.Client` respects the context deadline
edge_cases.error_condition: the context passed to `Exchange` or `UserInfo` is cancelled before the HTTP call completes

user_story.goal: configure OAuth providers in TOML and have them automatically registered at startup
user_story.benefit: so that no code changes are required to add a GitHub provider to a deployment

acceptance_criteria.action: is extended with an `OAuth OAuthConfig` field tagged `toml:"oauth"`, where `OAuthConfig` contains `Providers []ProviderConfig` tagged `toml:"providers"`, and `ProviderConfig` contains `Name`, `ClientID`, `ClientSecret`, `AuthorizeURL`, `TokenURL`, and `UserinfoURL` string fields with corresponding TOML tags
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: TOML config with `[[oauth.providers]]` blocks unmarshals correctly into `Config.OAuth.Providers`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns `(nil, error)` describing the missing field; the server does not start
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `(nil, error)` with a descriptive message
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a `[[oauth.providers]]` entry has an empty `name`, `client_id`, or `client_secret`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns `(nil, error)` indicating the duplicate provider name
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `(nil, error)`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: two or more `[[oauth.providers]]` entries share the same `name`

acceptance_criteria.action: iterates each `ProviderConfig`; for `name == "github"` creates a GitHub provider with the config's `ClientID`, `ClientSecret`, and optional URL overrides; registers each provider in a new `Registry`; for unknown provider names returns a startup error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Registry is populated; server proceeds to start. For unknown names, startup fails with an error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: server starts up with a valid config containing `[[oauth.providers]]` entries

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts the configuration as valid and the server starts normally; the registry contains no providers
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `(*Config, nil)`; `Registry.List()` returns `[]`
acceptance_criteria.state: the `[[oauth.providers]]` array is empty or absent in the config

edge_cases.action: returns a startup error naming the unsupported provider; the server does not start; no partial registry is exposed
edge_cases.return_contract: Startup returns error; process exits or returns error to caller without starting HTTP listener

user_story.goal: retrieve the list of configured OAuth providers and their authorization URLs
user_story.benefit: so that the CLI can present available login options and redirect the user to the correct authorization URL

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `Registry.List()` to get provider names in alphabetical order, then for each name calls `Registry.Get(name).AuthorizeURL("", "")` to obtain a base authorization URL with `client_id` and `scope` pre-populated but without `state` or `redirect_uri`, and returns a JSON array of objects each containing `name` and `authorize_url`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with `Content-Type: application/json`; body is a JSON array, e.g. `[{"name":"github","authorize_url":"https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize?client_id=...&scope=user:email"}]`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received at `{mount_point}/auth/providers`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an empty JSON array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200; body is `[]`
acceptance_criteria.state: no providers are registered in the `Registry`

acceptance_criteria.action: is registered with `CacheMiddleware(CachePublic)` so that responses carry `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response includes `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` header

acceptance_criteria.action: never includes `client_secret`, `token_url`, or `userinfo_url` in the response body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response JSON objects contain only `name` and `authorize_url` fields

user_story.goal: receive clear validation errors when the callback request is malformed
user_story.benefit: so that integration bugs are surfaced immediately with actionable messages

acceptance_criteria.action: proceeds to provider lookup without returning a validation error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: No 400 error from validation; processing continues
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request body is received with `provider`, `code`, and `redirect_uri` all non-empty and `expires` absent or one of `0`, `30`, `60`, `90`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an error response using `APIError()` with HTTP 400 and message `"provider is required"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400; `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"provider is required"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `provider` field is missing or empty in the request body

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an error response using `APIError()` with HTTP 400 and message `"code is required"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400; `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"code is required"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `code` field is missing or empty in the request body

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an error response using `APIError()` with HTTP 400 and message `"redirect_uri is required"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400; `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"redirect_uri is required"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `redirect_uri` field is missing or empty in the request body

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an error response using `APIError()` with HTTP 400 and message `"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400; `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `expires` field is present in the request body but not one of `0`, `30`, `60`, `90`

acceptance_criteria.action: defaults `expires` to `90`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Processing continues with `expires = 90`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `expires` field is absent from the request body

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 using `APIError()` with a message indicating invalid JSON or bad request
edge_cases.error_condition: the request body is not valid JSON

user_story.goal: validate the `redirect_uri` against a configured allowlist before exchanging the authorization code
user_story.benefit: so that open-redirect attacks are prevented

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts any `redirect_uri` matching `http://localhost:<port>/*` where `<port>` is any valid port number (1–65535), regardless of path
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns allowed (no error)

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts any `redirect_uri` whose scheme and host match the `external_url`'s scheme and host; the path may extend beyond the `external_url`'s path
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns allowed (no error)

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts only localhost URIs; non-localhost URIs are rejected
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns error for non-localhost URIs

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 using `APIError()` with message `"redirect_uri is not allowed"` without proceeding to code exchange
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400; `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"redirect_uri is not allowed"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `redirect_uri` does not match any entry in the allowlist

edge_cases.action: rejects the URI because only `http://localhost:*` is in the allowlist; HTTPS localhost is not accepted

edge_cases.error_condition: `redirect_uri` is a non-localhost HTTP URI when `external_url` is configured but the scheme or host does not match `external_url`

user_story.goal: exchange the authorization code for an access token from the identity provider
user_story.benefit: so that the server can retrieve user identity without persisting IdP tokens

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `provider.Exchange(ctx, code, redirectURI)` to obtain an access token from the identity provider
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: On success, `accessToken` is a non-empty string; processing continues to `UserInfo`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: provider lookup and redirect URI validation both succeed

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 401 using `APIError()` with message `"authorization code exchange failed"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 401; `{"error":{"code":401,"message":"authorization code exchange failed"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `provider.Exchange()` returns an error

edge_cases.action: the context cancellation propagates into `Exchange`; the method returns an error; the handler returns HTTP 401 without hanging
edge_cases.error_condition: the request context is cancelled while `provider.Exchange()` is in flight

user_story.goal: retrieve user identity fields from the OAuth provider after code exchange
user_story.benefit: so that the user record can be created or updated with current identity information

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `provider.UserInfo(ctx, accessToken)` to retrieve the user's identity
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: On success, returns a non-nil `*UserInfo` with at least `Username` and `ProviderID` populated
acceptance_criteria.trigger: code exchange succeeds and returns a non-empty `accessToken`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 502 using `APIError()` with message `"failed to retrieve user info from provider"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 502; `{"error":{"code":502,"message":"failed to retrieve user info from provider"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `provider.UserInfo()` returns an error

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 using `APIError()` with message `"provider returned empty email; email is required"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400; `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"provider returned empty email; email is required"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `provider.UserInfo()` returns a `*UserInfo` with an empty `Email` field

edge_cases.action: `provider.UserInfo()` returns a non-nil error; the handler returns HTTP 502 without retrying
edge_cases.error_condition: the identity provider's user info API returns HTTP 401 or 403 (token invalid or insufficient scope)

user_story.goal: create or update the user record in the database based on the identity provider's response
user_story.benefit: so that new users are onboarded and returning users have their profile kept current

acceptance_criteria.action: opens a database transaction via `db.WithTx` and queries the `users` table for an existing record matching `(provider, provider_id)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Transaction is open; query executes without error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: user info is retrieved successfully and email is non-empty

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a new UUID for the user ID; checks `admin_config` for `admin_email`; if the user's email matches `admin_email` and no user with `role = 'admin'` exists in `users`, sets `role = 'admin'`; otherwise sets `role = 'user'`; inserts the new user into `users` with `status = 'active'` and timestamps from `NowUTC()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: New row inserted in `users`; `id`, `username`, `email`, `provider`, `provider_id`, `role`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at` all populated
acceptance_criteria.trigger: no existing user matches `(provider, provider_id)` in the `users` table

acceptance_criteria.action: updates the existing user's `username`, `email`, and `updated_at` columns in `users`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `users` row updated; other fields (`id`, `role`, `status`, `created_at`) unchanged
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an existing user matches `(provider, provider_id)` in the `users` table and their `status` is not `blocked`

acceptance_criteria.action: rolls back the transaction and returns HTTP 403 using `APIError()` with message `"user is blocked"`; does not update the user record or issue a new key
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403; `{"error":{"code":403,"message":"user is blocked"}}`; database unchanged
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an existing user matches `(provider, provider_id)` and their `status` is `blocked`

edge_cases.action: `db.WithTx` rolls back the transaction automatically; the handler maps the error via `db.WrapError` and returns HTTP 500 using `APIError()` with message `"internal server error"`
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 500; transaction rolled back; no partial state in `users` or `api_keys`
edge_cases.error_condition: the database query or insert within `db.WithTx` returns an error

edge_cases.action: treats the condition as no admin email configured; sets new user's role to `'user'` and continues without error
edge_cases.return_contract: New user created with `role = 'user'`; no HTTP error
edge_cases.error_condition: `admin_email` does not exist in `admin_config` (table empty or row missing)

edge_cases.action: sets the new user's role to `'user'` (admin auto-grant only fires once, when no admin exists yet)
edge_cases.error_condition: a user with `role = 'admin'` already exists in `users` when a new user with a matching `admin_email` logs in for the first time

requirements.title: API Key Generation and Revocation on Callback

user_story.goal: generate a fresh cryptographically secure API key for the user on each login and revoke any previously active key
user_story.benefit: so that each login session has a unique credential and stale keys are invalidated

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `revoked_at = NowUTC()` on all active (non-revoked, non-expired) API keys for the user in `api_keys`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Previous active keys in `api_keys` have `revoked_at` set; no keys deleted
acceptance_criteria.trigger: user upsert succeeds inside the `db.WithTx` transaction

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a new `key_id` (random 8-character alphanumeric string via `crypto/rand`) and a new `secret` (random 32-character alphanumeric string via `crypto/rand`); computes SHA-256 hash of `secret`; calculates `expires_at` as `null` when `expires == 0`, or `NowUTC() + (expires * 24h)` otherwise; inserts the new key record into `api_keys` with `key_id`, `secret_hash`, `expires_at`, and the user's ID
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: New row in `api_keys` with correct fields; plaintext `secret` available in memory only
acceptance_criteria.trigger: previous key revocation succeeds

acceptance_criteria.action: uses `crypto/rand` (not `math/rand`) for all random generation of `key_id` and `secret` to ensure cryptographic security
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Key material is generated from a cryptographically secure random source

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the full API key in the response as `<TokenPrefix>_<key_id>_<secret>` where `TokenPrefix` is `apikit.TokenPrefix` (default `"ak"`); the plaintext `secret` is included exactly once in this response and is never stored
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response `api_key.key` matches pattern `ak_<8chars>_<32chars>`; `api_key.key_id` matches the 8-char segment; `api_key.expires_at` is RFC 3339 or null

edge_cases.action: returns an error from the key generation function; `db.WithTx` rolls back the transaction; handler returns HTTP 500
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 500; transaction rolled back; no key inserted in `api_keys`
edge_cases.error_condition: `crypto/rand` returns an error during `key_id` or `secret` generation

edge_cases.action: the revocation step is a no-op (zero rows updated); processing continues normally to key generation
edge_cases.return_contract: No error; new key inserted; HTTP 200
edge_cases.error_condition: the user has no active keys in `api_keys` at login time (e.g. first login)

user_story.goal: receive the full user object and new API key in a single response after successful authentication
user_story.benefit: so that the CLI can store the API key and display the user's identity without additional requests

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing a `user` object and an `api_key` object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200; `Content-Type: application/json`; body matches schema `{"user":{"id":"<uuid>","username":"<string>","email":"<string>","full_name":"<string>","status":"active","role":"<string>","provider":"<string>","provider_id":"<string>","created_at":"<RFC3339>","updated_at":"<RFC3339>"},"api_key":{"key":"<prefix>_<key_id>_<secret>","key_id":"<key_id>","expires_at":"<RFC3339 or null>"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: all processing steps (validation, exchange, user info, upsert, key generation) complete successfully and the transaction is committed

acceptance_criteria.action: includes the `role` field in the `user` object of the response
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `user.role` is either `"admin"` or `"user"`

acceptance_criteria.action: formats all timestamps in the response using `FormatUTC()` producing RFC 3339 strings; `api_key.expires_at` is `null` (JSON null) when `expires == 0`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Timestamps are valid RFC 3339 strings or JSON null

requirements.title: Handler Registration and HTTP Client Configuration

user_story.goal: register OAuth handlers on the Echo group and configure a shared HTTP client for providers
user_story.benefit: so that the endpoints are live and providers share a single connection pool with a bounded timeout

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `RegisterOAuthHandlers(group *echo.Group, registry *oauth.Registry, database *db.DB, externalURL string)` to mount `GET /auth/providers` with `CacheMiddleware(CachePublic)` and `POST /auth/callback` (inheriting the group's default `Cache-Control: no-store`) on the Echo group returned by `APIGroup()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Both routes are reachable; `GET /auth/providers` responds with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`; `POST /auth/callback` has no public caching

acceptance_criteria.action: creates a single `*http.Client` with a 30-second timeout using `http.DefaultTransport` and injects it into all provider instances at construction time; providers do not create their own HTTP clients
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All provider HTTP calls use the shared `*http.Client`; timeout is 30 seconds

user_story.goal: handle concurrent OAuth callback requests for the same user without data corruption
user_story.benefit: so that simultaneous logins do not leave the user with multiple active keys or inconsistent state

acceptance_criteria.action: relies on SQLite's single-connection serialization (enforced by `SetMaxOpenConns(1)` in the `database_layer`) to serialize concurrent transactions; no application-level locks or serializable isolation hints are used
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Concurrent requests execute sequentially at the database layer; no deadlock or data corruption

acceptance_criteria.action: the second transaction observes the first transaction's new key and revokes it before issuing yet another key; the user ends up with exactly one active key after both transactions complete
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: After both transactions, exactly one active (non-revoked) key exists for the user in `api_keys`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: two sequential transactions process OAuth callbacks for the same user

user_story.goal: remove an OAuth provider from config without affecting existing user credentials
user_story.benefit: so that removing a provider does not break active user sessions or audit trails

acceptance_criteria.action: retains all existing user records in `users` with their original `provider` and `provider_id` values; no database cleanup is performed; existing API keys remain valid until they expire or are revoked
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: No rows deleted from `users` or `api_keys`; existing credentials continue to authenticate
acceptance_criteria.state: a provider has been removed from the `[[oauth.providers]]` config and the server restarted

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"unknown provider: <name>"` because the provider is no longer in the registry
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400; `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"unknown provider: <name>"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a callback request references a provider name that was removed from config

correctness_properties.title: API Key Uniqueness and Revocation Atomicity
correctness_properties.invariant: there is exactly one active (non-revoked, non-expired) key in `api_keys` for the user after the transaction commits; all previously active keys have `revoked_at` set

correctness_properties.title: Blocked User Status Immutability
correctness_properties.for_any: POST /auth/callback request for a user with `status = 'blocked'` in `users`
correctness_properties.invariant: the user's `status` remains `'blocked'` after the request; no new key is created in `api_keys`; HTTP 403 is returned

correctness_properties.title: Secret Non-Persistence
correctness_properties.invariant: the plaintext `secret` is never written to the database; only the SHA-256 hash is stored in `secret_hash`; the plaintext appears exactly once in the HTTP 200 response

correctness_properties.title: Admin Auto-Grant Fires At Most Once
correctness_properties.for_any: any number of OAuth callback requests from a user whose email matches `admin_email`
correctness_properties.invariant: the `role = 'admin'` grant is applied at most once — only when no user with `role = 'admin'` exists in `users` at insert time; subsequent logins do not change the role of any existing user

correctness_properties.title: Provider Registry Name Uniqueness
correctness_properties.for_any: any sequence of `Register()` calls on a `Registry`
correctness_properties.invariant: no two providers with the same name can coexist in the `Registry`; `Register()` returns a non-nil error for duplicates without replacing the existing provider

correctness_properties.title: No Secrets in Provider List Response
correctness_properties.invariant: the response body contains only `name` and `authorize_url` fields per provider entry; `client_secret`, `token_url`, and `userinfo_url` are never included

correctness_properties.title: Redirect URI Allowlist Enforcement
correctness_properties.invariant: `provider.Exchange()` is never called when `redirect_uri` fails allowlist validation; the request is rejected with HTTP 400 before any identity provider communication occurs

correctness_properties.title: Transaction Atomicity for User Upsert and Key Creation
correctness_properties.for_any: POST /auth/callback request that progresses to the database phase
correctness_properties.invariant: the user upsert and key creation (including old key revocation) are executed within a single `db.WithTx` transaction; if any step fails, the entire transaction is rolled back and neither partial user updates nor partial key records are visible

execution_paths.title: Full OAuth Callback Flow — New User Happy Path

steps.action: sends POST to `{mount_point}/auth/callback` with JSON body containing `provider`, `code`, `redirect_uri`, and optional `expires`
parses and validates request body; rejects with HTTP 400 if any required field is missing or `expires` is invalid
looks up provider by name in the `Registry`; rejects with HTTP 400 if not found
checks `redirect_uri` against localhost allowlist and `external_url`; rejects with HTTP 400 if not allowed
calls `Exchange(ctx, code, redirectURI)` which POSTs to `token_url` with credentials and returns an access token; rejects with HTTP 401 on failure
calls `UserInfo(ctx, accessToken)` which GETs `userinfo_url` and returns a `*UserInfo` with `Username`, `Email`, and `ProviderID`; rejects with HTTP 502 on failure
validates that `UserInfo.Email` is non-empty; rejects with HTTP 400 if empty
opens a transaction; queries `users` by `(provider, provider_id)`; finds no match; checks `admin_config` for `admin_email`; inserts new user into `users` with appropriate `role`

revokes all active keys in `api_keys` for the user (no-op for new user); generates new `key_id` and `secret` via `crypto/rand`; computes SHA-256 hash; inserts new row in `api_keys`; commits transaction
returns HTTP 200 with JSON body containing the full `user` object and `api_key` including plaintext secret
receives HTTP 200 response; stores the API key for subsequent authenticated requests

execution_paths.title: Full OAuth Callback Flow — Returning User Re-Login

steps.action: sends POST to `{mount_point}/auth/callback` with valid `provider`, `code`, `redirect_uri`
validates request, looks up provider in `Registry`, validates `redirect_uri`
exchanges code for access token via `Exchange()`; retrieves user info via `UserInfo()`
opens transaction; queries `users` by `(provider, provider_id)`; finds existing user with `status = 'active'`; updates `username`, `email`, `updated_at`
sets `revoked_at = NowUTC()` on the user's existing active key in `api_keys`; generates new `key_id` and `secret`; inserts new key record; commits transaction
returns HTTP 200 with updated `user` object and new `api_key`; previous key is now revoked
sends GET to `{mount_point}/auth/providers` with no authentication
sets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` on the response
calls `Registry.List()` to get alphabetically ordered provider names; for each name calls `Registry.Get(name).AuthorizeURL("", "")` to build the base authorization URL
returns HTTP 200 with JSON array of `{name, authorize_url}` objects; no secrets included
receives list of providers; selects a provider; appends `state` and `redirect_uri` to `authorize_url`; opens browser to the constructed URL

execution_paths.title: Blocked User Rejection on OAuth Callback

steps.action: sends POST to `{mount_point}/auth/callback` with valid credentials for a blocked user
validates request, looks up provider, validates `redirect_uri`, exchanges code, retrieves user info
opens transaction; queries `users` by `(provider, provider_id)`; finds existing user with `status = 'blocked'`; rolls back transaction
returns HTTP 403 using `APIError()` with message `"user is blocked"`; no key is generated or revoked
receives HTTP 403 response; displays error to user

calls `LoadConfig()` from spec `01_server_core` which reads and validates `[[oauth.providers]]` TOML entries including non-empty `name`, `client_id`, `client_secret`, and uniqueness; fails fast with error if any validation fails

creates a new `Registry` via `NewRegistry()`; iterates `Config.OAuth.Providers`; for each entry with `name == "github"` creates a GitHub provider with `ClientID`, `ClientSecret`, and optional URL overrides; injects the shared 30-second-timeout `*http.Client`
calls `Registry.Register(provider)` for each provider; returns startup error for unknown provider names
calls `RegisterOAuthHandlers(APIGroup(), registry, db, externalURL)` to mount `GET /auth/providers` and `POST /auth/callback` on the Echo group
begins serving traffic; both OAuth endpoints are reachable; `GET /auth/providers` returns the configured provider list

error_handling.condition: `provider` field is missing or empty in POST /auth/callback request body
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 via `APIError()` with message `"provider is required"`

error_handling.condition: `code` field is missing or empty in POST /auth/callback request body
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 via `APIError()` with message `"code is required"`

error_handling.condition: `redirect_uri` field is missing or empty in POST /auth/callback request body
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 via `APIError()` with message `"redirect_uri is required"`

error_handling.condition: `expires` field is present but not one of `0`, `30`, `60`, `90`
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 via `APIError()` with message `"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"`

error_handling.condition: Provider name in request does not match any registered provider in the `Registry`
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 via `APIError()` with message `"unknown provider: <name>"`

error_handling.condition: `redirect_uri` does not match localhost allowlist or `external_url`
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 via `APIError()` with message `"redirect_uri is not allowed"` before calling `Exchange()`

error_handling.condition: `provider.Exchange()` returns an error (e.g. invalid code, network failure, timeout)
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 401 via `APIError()` with message `"authorization code exchange failed"`

error_handling.condition: `provider.UserInfo()` returns an error (e.g. network failure, identity provider API error)
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 502 via `APIError()` with message `"failed to retrieve user info from provider"`

error_handling.condition: `UserInfo.Email` is empty after successful user info retrieval
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 via `APIError()` with message `"provider returned empty email; email is required"`
Transaction is rolled back; handler returns HTTP 403 via `APIError()` with message `"user is blocked"`; user status is not changed

error_handling.condition: Database error occurs during user upsert or key creation within `db.WithTx`
error_handling.behavior: `db.WithTx` rolls back the transaction; handler maps error via `db.WrapError` and returns HTTP 500 via `APIError()` with message `"internal server error"`

error_handling.condition: `crypto/rand` returns an error during key material generation
error_handling.behavior: Key generation function returns error; `db.WithTx` rolls back the transaction; handler returns HTTP 500

error_handling.condition: A `[[oauth.providers]]` entry in config is missing `name`, `client_id`, or `client_secret`
error_handling.behavior: `LoadConfig()` returns `(nil, error)` describing the missing field; server does not start
`LoadConfig()` returns `(nil, error)` indicating the duplicate; server does not start
Server bootstrap returns a startup error naming the unsupported provider; server does not start; no partial registry is exposed

symbols.name: Group
symbols.notes: Echo router group used for mounting OAuth handlers via RegisterOAuthHandlers

symbols.name: New
symbols.notes: Generates a new random UUID for new user IDs

symbols.name: Sum256
symbols.notes: Computes SHA-256 hash of API key secret before storage in `secret_hash`

symbols.name: Read
symbols.notes: Cryptographically secure random bytes source used to generate `key_id` and `secret`

symbols.name: Client
symbols.signature: type Client struct { Transport RoundTripper; Timeout time.Duration; ... }
symbols.notes: Shared HTTP client with 30-second timeout injected into provider instances

symbols.name: DefaultTransport
symbols.notes: Used as the transport for the shared HTTP client
